meta.title: Watch SpaceX launch the Cygnus Spacecraft to the ISS for NASA!
meta.description:
Northrop Grumman’s Cygnus spacecraft performs a Commercial Resupply Services (CRS) mission for NASA, NG-20, this time aboard SpaceX’s Falcon 9 launch vehicle for the first time ever. Liftoff takes place from Space Launch Complex 40 (SLC-40), at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station, Florida. Falcon 9’s booster will return for a touchdown on Landing Zone 1.

Read more about it with our Prelaunch Preview - https://everydayastronaut.com/crs2-cygnus-ng-20-falcon-block-5/

00:00 - Intro
03:12 - Prelaunch Preview
16:00 - Q&A
20:30 - Stream Starts
22:12 - (Audio Fixed)
23:00 - Stream
27:10 - T - 30 seconds
37:40 - Liftoff
40:00 - Stage Sep
44:29 - Entry Burn
44:46 - Tracking shot!!!
45:54 - Landing!
49:20 - Reentry Explanation
52:25 - Cygnus separation
55:31 - Replay of landing
59:10 - Q&A

--------------------------
